seo.title: Building Agentic RAG with Instructor + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build agentic rag with Instructor + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Instructor simplifies structured output extraction from LLMs by patching clients to return validated Pydantic models, perfect for Agentic RAG systems that plan queries and synthesize multi-source retrievals. Guardrails are essential in this high-risk use case to prevent hallucinated citations, inconsistent query plans, and unfaithful source attribution that erode trust. Vex provides runtime verification to catch these failures before they propagate through your agent's reasoning chain.

architectureOverview.heading: Agentic RAG Architecture with Instructor
architectureOverview.description:
Agentic RAG extends traditional retrieval-augmented generation with autonomous query planning and multi-step synthesis. The agent receives a user question, plans optimal retrieval queries using Instructor-patched LLM calls to extract structured QueryPlan objects, executes parallel retrievals from vector stores and knowledge bases, then synthesizes a final response with verified citations.

Instructor ensures each step produces validated outputs: QueryPlan models guarantee complete query coverage, RetrievalResult schemas enforce source attribution, and FinalAnswer models validate citation integrity. This structured approach prevents partial extractions and schema violations common in agentic workflows.

The architecture forms a feedback loop where verification failures trigger replanning, ensuring high-fidelity responses even with complex multi-hop queries across diverse sources.

risks.name: Source Faithfulness Drift
risks.description: Agent synthesizes information not present in retrieved sources, creating plausible but ungrounded claims
risks.mitigation: Vex semantic similarity checks between response claims and source chunks

risks.name: Query Planning Gaps
risks.description: Incomplete or misaligned query plans miss critical information needed for comprehensive answers
risks.mitigation: Instructor validation + Vex query coverage scoring against user intent

risks.name: Cross-Source Inconsistency
risks.description: Conflicting information across sources handled inconsistently, favoring recency over accuracy
risks.mitigation: Vex consistency scoring across RetrievalResult objects

risks.name: Citation Hallucination
risks.description: Fake or incorrect source references undermine response credibility
risks.mitigation: Vex citation verification linking claims to exact source chunks

implementation.title: Query Planning with Instructor
implementation.description: Extract structured QueryPlan from agent reasoning using Instructor patching
implementation.code:
from instructor import from_openai
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import List

class QueryPlan(BaseModel):
    queries: List[str] = Field(..., description="Retrieval queries to execute")
    rationale: str = Field(..., description="Why these queries cover the question")

client = from_openai(OpenAI())

query_plan = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-4o",
    response_model=QueryPlan,
    messages=[
        {"role": "system", "content": "Plan optimal retrieval queries for RAG"},
        {"role": "user", "content": "What are the key features of Agentic RAG?"}
    ]
)

print(query_plan.model_dump_json(indent=2))

implementation.title: Multi-Source Retrieval Results
implementation.description: Validate retrieval chunks with source attribution using Instructor models
implementation.code:
from instructor import from_openai
from pydantic import BaseModel
from typing import List

class RetrievalResult(BaseModel):
    content: str
    source_id: str
    source_type: str
    relevance_score: float
    chunk_id: str

class RetrievalBatch(BaseModel):
    results: List[RetrievalResult]
    query: str

client = from_openai(OpenAI())
retrieval_batch = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-4o",
    response_model=RetrievalBatch,
    messages=[
        {"role": "user", "content": "Process these chunks: [chunk1, chunk2, chunk3]"}
    ]
)

implementation.title: TypeScript Query Planning
implementation.description: Instructor.js equivalent for TypeScript structured extraction

implementation.title: Final Answer Synthesis
implementation.description: Extract verified final answer with citations using Instructor
implementation.code:
from instructor import from_openai
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import List, Optional

class Citation(BaseModel):
    claim: str
    source_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    confidence: float

class FinalAnswer(BaseModel):
    answer: str = Field(..., max_length=2000)
    citations: List[Citation] = Field(..., min_items=1)
    confidence: float
    unanswered_aspects: Optional[List[str]] = None

client = from_openai(OpenAI())
final_answer = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-4o",
    response_model=FinalAnswer,
    messages=[
        {"role": "system", "content": "Synthesize with source attribution"},
        {"role": "user", "content": "Synthesize from these retrieval results: ..."}
    ]
)

cta.description: Add Vex verification to your Instructor agent in 5 minutes - start with `pip install vex-sdk` and protect against hallucination, citation drift, and planning failures today.
